title: Insufficient Email Security and User Awareness
description: Learn how weak phishing protection and lack of user awareness can allow attackers to compromise systems through malicious emails.

attack_stages.title: Phishing Delivery
attack_stages.description: Attackers send fraudulent emails or messages to employees.

attack_stages.title: User Interaction
attack_stages.description: A user unknowingly clicks a malicious link or opens an infected attachment.

attack_stages.title: Malware Execution
attack_stages.description: Malicious code is executed on the system.

attack_stages.title: Ransomware Activation
attack_stages.description: Files and databases are encrypted and held for ransom.

attack_stages.title: Data Compromise
attack_stages.description: Sensitive organizational data becomes inaccessible or stolen.

prevention_points.title: Email Filtering Systems
prevention_points.description: Use spam filters and phishing detection tools to block malicious emails.

prevention_points.title: Security Awareness Training
prevention_points.description: Train employees to recognize phishing attempts and suspicious attachments.

prevention_points.title: Anti-Malware Protection
prevention_points.description: Deploy endpoint security tools to detect and block malicious software.

prevention_points.title: Data Backups & Isolation
prevention_points.description: Maintain regular backups and isolate critical systems to limit damage.

nodes.title: Scenario
nodes.text: A company relies on email communication for internal operations. Employees frequently receive external emails containing attachments and links.

nodes.title: What is Phishing?
nodes.text: Phishing is a type of cyber attack where attackers send fraudulent emails or messages designed to trick users into revealing sensitive information or executing malicious actions.

nodes.title: Weak Email Security
nodes.text: Weak email security occurs when organizations fail to properly filter malicious messages or adequately train users to recognize phishing attempts.

nodes.title: Attacker Goal
nodes.text: The attacker wants to trick an employee into opening a malicious email that will allow them to compromise the system.

nodes.title: What is the most likely initial success point for a phishing attack?

choices.text: A user clicks a malicious link or opens an infected attachment
The firewall automatically blocks all emails
The database rejects all external connections

nodes.title: Try Again
nodes.text: Phishing attacks often succeed because users interact with malicious emails, not because systems automatically block everything.

nodes.title: Malware Execution
nodes.text: The user unknowingly executes malicious code through a downloaded file or link, allowing the attacker to gain a foothold in the system.

nodes.title: Ransomware Attack
nodes.text: The malware spreads and encrypts critical database files, making them inaccessible. The attacker demands payment in exchange for recovery.

nodes.title: Business Impact
nodes.text: Ransomware can disrupt operations, cause financial loss, and lead to permanent data loss if backups are not available.

nodes.title: How to Prevent This
nodes.text: Organizations should invest in employee awareness training, deploy email filtering systems, use anti-malware tools, maintain regular backups, and isolate critical systems to limit infection spread.

nodes.title: Attack Replay
nodes.text: Review how a simple phishing email led to malware execution, ransomware infection, and eventual compromise of sensitive database systems.

nodes.title: Scenario Complete
nodes.text: Email-based attacks remain one of the most effective entry points for cybercriminals. User awareness and strong email security controls are essential to prevent ransomware and malware infections.
